facts_srcauth_719089488799.content: getAuthService() was a private module-level singleton in withAuth.ts — had to export it for route handler reuse

conventions_srcappapi_719090865231.content: Route handlers return new Response(JSON.stringify(...), {status, headers}) — no framework wrappers, pure Next.js Response

thoughts_srcauth_719092460176.content: AuthService.login() throws {message, status} errors; catch err.status to set HTTP response — no Result type needed at route boundary

conventions_srcappapi_719093771913.content: Test files are co-located next to route files as *.test.ts, using vi.useFakeTimers() and NextRequest construction

conventions_srcappapi_719095169427.content: CORS headers are present on other routes and should be included on new auth routes for consistency

conventions_auth_986741041647.content: Reuse `getAuthService()` singleton from `withAuth.ts` instead of instantiating `JwtService` directly — it manages the singleton lifecycle.

thoughts_srcappapi_986742656031.content: `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` in the OPTIONS handler conflicts with the same-origin default used elsewhere in the codebase.

facts_auth_986744600131.content: Routing through `AuthService.login()` (PBKDF2/Payload) vs old `src/api/auth/login.ts` (SHA-256/UserStore) intentionally avoids dual-auth anti-pattern.

facts_auth_986745800414.content: Old `src/api/auth/login.ts` utility was intentionally left untouched since the task scope only covered adding the new route handler.
